title: clinopodium alpinum subsp. orontium
intro: Clinopodium alpinum subsp. orontium, also known as alpine calamint or mountain calamint, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which make it useful for treating a range of health issues including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The plant has been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting digestion, due to its high concentration of essential oils like borneol and camphor. In herbal preparations, Clinopodium alpinum subsp. orontium is often used as a tea or tincture, and can also be applied topically in the form of a salve or oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
